pittler2012.author: Pittler, M.H. and Ernst, E.
pittler2012.title: Horse chestnut seed extract for chronic venous insufficiency

cheong2018.author: Cheong, D.H.J., Arfuso, F., Sethi, G., Wang, L., Hui, K.M., Kumar, A.P. and Tran, T.
cheong2018.title: Molecular targets and anti-cancer potential of escin

actions.action: Chronic venous insufficiency - reduces leg pain, swelling (oedema), heaviness and itching; supports varicose veins

constituents.name: Triterpene saponins (escin / aescin)
constituents.note: The active mixture responsible for the venotonic and anti-oedematous effects; extracts are standardised to it.

contraindications.note: Use only standardised, processed seed extract. Raw conkers, leaves and bark contain toxic esculin and can cause poisoning - never eat raw horse chestnut.
May increase bleeding risk, so use caution with anticoagulant/antiplatelet medicines; use caution in kidney disease and avoid in pregnancy and breastfeeding.

internal_notes: Enriched 2026-06-12 from scientific literature (Cochrane review of horse chestnut seed extract for CVI; Cancer Letters review of escin). Originally a skeleton imported from the omniasana.bio Symptom-to-Plant Lookup tool (varicose-veins, swelling, hemorrhoids, inflammation); associations retained and now part-mapped and cited.

kruttschnitt2020.author: Kruttschnitt, E., Wegener, T., Zahner, C. and Henzen-Bucking, S.
kruttschnitt2020.title: Assessment of the Efficacy and Safety of Ivy Leaf Cough Syrup Compared with Acetylcysteine in Adults and Children with Acute Bronchitis

schonknecht2017.author: Schonknecht, K., Fal, A.M., Mastalerz-Migas, A., Joachimiak, M. and Doniec, Z.
schonknecht2017.title: Efficacy of dry extract of ivy leaves in the treatment of productive cough

actions.action: Expectorant / secretolytic for productive cough and acute bronchitis (loosens and helps clear mucus)
Antispasmodic / bronchodilatory - eases coughing fits and breathlessness

constituents.name: Triterpene saponins (hederacoside C, alpha-hederin)
constituents.note: The secretolytic and antispasmodic active constituents; extracts are standardised to them.

contraindications.note: Generally well tolerated; occasional gastrointestinal upset or, rarely, allergic reaction. Use only standardised extract products - the fresh raw plant contains irritant saponins and falcarinol that can cause contact dermatitis.
A cough lasting more than a week, or with fever, breathlessness or purulent sputum, needs medical assessment. Safety in pregnancy and breastfeeding is not established.

internal_notes: Enriched 2026-06-12 from scientific literature (two clinical effectiveness studies of standardised ivy-leaf extract in acute bronchitis / productive cough). Originally a skeleton imported from the omniasana.bio Symptom-to-Plant Lookup tool (cough, bronchitis); associations retained and now part-mapped and cited.

bahramsoltani2018.author: Bahramsoltani, R., Rostamiasrabadi, P., Shahpiri, Z., Marques, A.M., Rahimi, R. and Farzaei, M.H.
bahramsoltani2018.title: Aloysia citrodora Palau (Lemon verbena): A review of phytochemistry and pharmacology

carmona2019.author: Carmona, F., Coneglian, F.S., Batista, P.A., Aragon, D.C., Angelucci, M.A., Martinez, E.Z. and Pereira, A.M.S.
carmona2019.title: Aloysia polystachya powdered leaves are effective in treating anxiety symptoms: A phase-2, randomized, placebo-controlled clinical trial

actions.action: Anxiolytic / calming (anxiety) - also supported by a clinical trial of the closely related Aloysia polystachya
Antioxidant - supports muscle recovery and reduces exercise-induced muscle soreness and oxidative stress

constituents.name: Essential oil (neral and geranial, i.e. citral)
constituents.note: Gives the lemon aroma and contributes to the calming and antimicrobial effects.

constituents.name: Verbascoside (acteoside)
constituents.note: The principal polyphenol of the extract and a major antioxidant constituent.

contraindications.note: Generally well tolerated; human safety and efficacy are not fully established. Its sedative effect can add to that of alcohol, sedatives and sleep medicines.
Safety in pregnancy and breastfeeding is not established, so avoid medicinal doses.

internal_notes: Enriched 2026-06-12 from scientific literature (Journal of Ethnopharmacology review of A. citrodora; congeneric anxiety RCT of A. polystachya). Originally a skeleton imported from the omniasana.bio Symptom-to-Plant Lookup tool (insomnia, anxiety, indigestion, muscle-soreness, inflammation); associations retained and now part-mapped and cited.

actions.action: Traditional 'alterative' / depurative for chronic skin conditions - eczema, psoriasis, acne and boils (internally and topically)
Sebaceous-gland regulator - helps balance skin oil (acne)

constituents.name: Inulin (prebiotic fructan, up to ~50% of the root)
constituents.note: The major storage carbohydrate; a prebiotic fibre.

constituents.name: Polyphenolic acids (caffeic, chlorogenic), polyacetylenes and tannins

contraindications.note: As a member of the daisy family (Asteraceae), it can cause allergic reactions in people sensitive to ragweed and related plants. The high inulin content can cause temporary bloating or gas.
May lower blood sugar (caution with antidiabetic medicines) and add to diuretics; avoid medicinal doses in pregnancy.

internal_notes: Enriched 2026-06-12 from general phytotherapy literature (Herbal Reality and Drugs.com monographs). Medicinal burdock is usually Arctium lappa; A. minus is used similarly. The book database already contains A. lappa (Greater Burdock). Originally a skeleton imported from the omniasana.bio Symptom-to-Plant Lookup tool (eczema, acne, skin-irritation, detox); associations retained and now part-mapped and cited.

ema.author: European Medicines Agency (HMPC)
ema.title: Community herbal monograph on Tilia cordata Miller, Tilia platyphyllos Scop., Tilia x vulgaris Heyne or their mixtures, flos (Tiliae flos)

actions.action: Diaphoretic infusion for feverish colds and flu (promotes sweating to support a fever)
Relief of mild mental stress and to aid sleep (gentle calming)
Demulcent for cough and catarrh (soothing mucilage)

constituents.name: Flavonoids (quercetin and kaempferol glycosides, tiliroside)
constituents.note: Antioxidant constituents associated with the diaphoretic and calming effects.

constituents.name: Volatile oil (with farnesol), phenolic acids and tannins

contraindications.note: Generally very well tolerated, including (per the EU herbal monograph) in children. A feverish cold that persists or worsens, or a high or prolonged fever, needs medical assessment.
Use in pregnancy and breastfeeding is not recommended in the absence of adequate data.

internal_notes: Enriched 2026-06-12 from regulatory literature (EMA HMPC monograph for Tiliae flos, covering T. cordata with T. platyphyllos and T. x vulgaris). Originally a skeleton imported from the omniasana.bio Symptom-to-Plant Lookup tool (anxiety, insomnia, cold, fever, stress); associations retained and now part-mapped and cited.
